primary_diagnosis.name: Mild Crohn's disease flare (ileocaecal), appendicitis not excludable remotely

pathophysiology_summary: Terminal-ileal/ileocaecal Crohn's inflammation produces colicky RLQ pain, loose stools and low-grade temperature, and can closely mimic appendicitis — sharing the central-to-RLQ migration pattern. Reassuring features are colicky (not constant) pain, a soft non-peritonitic abdomen, preserved appetite, no high fever and prior similar episodes. However, appendicitis cannot be excluded without abdominal examination (localised guarding, rebound, Rovsing's) and, often, inflammatory markers/imaging — none available via telehealth. Safe management therefore treats the flare symptomatically only alongside urgent in-person assessment and a strict peritonism safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Colicky migrating RLQ pain with loose stools and a history of episodic ileocaecal symptoms.
Central-to-RLQ migration is the classic appendicitis prodrome; cannot be excluded without examination.
Screen for loin-to-groin colicky pain and restlessness; not the pattern here.
Consider post-viral adenitis; no preceding URTI reported.
Colicky (not constant) pain, soft abdomen, preserved appetite, no high fever (DI-001, DI-002), migrating RLQ pain with loose stools and prior episodes (DI-003) favour a flare.
Reassuring history lowers probability but appendicitis cannot be excluded remotely; retain urgent in-person assessment.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: symptomatic care (simple analgesia/antispasmodic, gut rest) PLUS urgent same-day in-person assessment because RLQ pain cannot be examined remotely, and a strict peritonism safety-net. Confidently reassuring 'just a flare' remotely is unsafe.

red_flags.description: Constant severe pain, high fever, anorexia or persistent vomiting (appendicitis/surgical abdomen)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Would indicate appendicitis or another surgical cause.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed appendicitis progresses to perforation and peritonitis.

red_flags.description: Peritonism — pain markedly worse on movement/coughing, rebound, or a rigid abdomen
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Delayed recognition of perforation causes sepsis.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Attributing localising RLQ pain to a Crohn's flare and reassuring remotely without excluding appendicitis by examination.
Treating a reassuring history as equivalent to an abdominal examination.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Abdominal palpation (localised guarding, rebound, Rovsing's, McBurney's)
Inflammatory markers (CRP, faecal calprotectin) and imaging
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Urgent same-day in-person assessment (GP/urgent care/ED) with a strict peritonism safety-net; symptomatic relief meanwhile.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Labelling as a flare and reassuring remotely risks missing appendicitis; over-escalating every flare to ED wastes resources — same-day in-person assessment balances these.
